compatibleScales.relationship: Primary major scale of the key center
compatibleScales.rationale: The fundamental major scale built on Bb as the tonic. Contains all seven diatonic notes that define Bb major harmony and establishes the key center
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal major tonality; essential for classical, pop, rock, jazz, and all Western music. The foundation for understanding all other scales in this key. Bb is one of the most common keys for wind instruments and jazz
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use as the primary scale for Bb major compositions and improvisations. Essential for establishing strong tonal center and major key feeling
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Folk, Jazz, Classical

compatibleScales.relationship: Primary minor scale of the key center
compatibleScales.rationale: The fundamental natural minor scale built on Bb as the tonic. Relative major is Db major, sharing the same key signature but with Bb as the tonal center
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal minor tonality; works across all genres from classical to rock, metal, and pop. Creates melancholic, introspective, or dark emotional character
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Primary choice for Bb minor compositions. Use over Bbm chord progressions, especially i-iv-v. Essential for natural minor harmony

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset - scale tones 1, 2, 3, 5, 6 only
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains only the most consonant notes of Bb major (Bb, C, D, F, G), avoiding the 4th and 7th which create tension. Completely safe for any Bb major harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all genres; creates bright, optimistic melodies. Especially effective in rock, blues, country, folk, and pop music
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for improvisation with guaranteed consonance. Works over I-IV-V progressions in Bb major. Ideal for creating singable melodies and memorable hooks

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic built on tonic
compatibleScales.rationale: The most universal minor scale containing Bb, Db, Eb, F, Ab. Avoids the 2nd and 6th scale degrees, providing pure minor character without dissonance
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Essential for rock, blues, metal, and funk. The foundation of blues-rock guitar solos and the most commonly used scale in rock music
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Go-to scale for Bb minor improvisation. Works perfectly over Bbm chord vamps and i-iv-v progressions. Universal minor sound that never clashes
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Funk, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic with added b5 blue note
compatibleScales.rationale: Adds the characteristic blue note (E natural/Fb) to the minor pentatonic, creating the definitive blues/rock sound. The b5 creates tension that resolves to the perfect 5th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Essential for blues, rock, jazz-blues fusion, and funk. The signature sound of blues guitar and piano from B.B. King to Herbie Hancock
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bb dominant 7th or minor 7th chords. The E natural blue note is the key tension note - use it as a passing tone or bend into it
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Funk, Jazz Blues

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor mode with major 6th (G)
compatibleScales.rationale: Natural minor with raised 6th degree, creating a sophisticated minor sound that's brighter than Aeolian. The major 6th provides a jazz/funk characteristic
compatibleScales.musicalContext: The go-to minor scale for jazz, funk, fusion, and contemporary styles. More versatile than natural minor due to the major 6th. Used extensively by Miles Davis and Herbie Hancock
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Primary choice for modern minor improvisation. Works beautifully over Bbm7 and Ebm7 chords. Perfect for modal vamps and ii-V progressions in minor

compatibleScales.relationship: Natural minor with raised 7th degree
compatibleScales.rationale: Natural minor with major 7th (A natural), creating the characteristic augmented 2nd interval between Gb and A. Provides strong V7-i resolution in minor keys
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical music, neoclassical metal, flamenco, Middle Eastern music. The signature minor scale of Bach and Yngwie Malmsteen
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential for classical minor harmony and V7-i cadences. Use over Bbm with F7 dominant chords. The A natural leading tone creates strong pull to tonic

compatibleScales.relationship: Natural minor with raised 6th and 7th degrees
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor scale with both G and A (major 6th and 7th), creating a smooth melodic line. More consonant than harmonic minor while maintaining minor character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, modern classical. The foundation of jazz minor harmony and the source of altered dominant scales. Essential for contemporary improvisation
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Primary jazz minor scale. Use ascending over Bbm(maj7) chords. Opens up the entire melodic minor modal system for advanced improvisation
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Major scale with b7 - dominant 7th character
compatibleScales.rationale: Major scale with Ab instead of A, creating the dominant 7th chord sound. The b7 provides bluesy, funky, or rock character to the major scale
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, funk, folk; dominant 7th chord vamps. Signature sound of The Beatles, Grateful Dead, Allman Brothers. Essential for Southern rock and jam bands
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect over Bb7 chords and I7 vamps. Use for blues-rock solos with major tonality. The Ab is the characteristic note to emphasize
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Country, Southern Rock, Jam Band

compatibleScales.relationship: Major scale with raised 4th degree (#4)
compatibleScales.rationale: Bb major with E natural instead of Eb, creating a bright, ethereal, dreamlike quality. The #4 creates characteristic Lydian color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores, progressive rock, fusion. The signature sound of Joe Satriani, Steve Vai, and John Williams film scores. Creates uplifting, floating feeling
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bbmaj7 or Bbmaj7#11 chords for modal compositions. Perfect for I chord vamps and impressionistic harmony. Emphasize the E natural for Lydian character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Score, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Dream Pop

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor scale with b2 - Spanish/flamenco character
compatibleScales.rationale: Natural minor with Cb (B natural) instead of C, creating the characteristic flamenco/Spanish sound. The b2 creates exotic, dark, Spanish character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, metal, progressive rock, Middle Eastern music. Essential for Phrygian dominant sound in metal (Metallica, Testament). Creates dark, mysterious atmosphere
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bbm or Bbsus chords for Spanish or metal flavors. Emphasize the Cb for Phrygian character. Perfect for riffs in Phrygian dominant style
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Metal, Progressive Rock, Middle Eastern

compatibleScales.relationship: Relative major - shares all notes with Bb minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Db major is the relative major of Bb minor, containing the exact same notes but centered on Db. This relationship is fundamental to understanding major/minor duality
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all genres. Understanding relative relationships is essential for modulation and understanding how major and minor keys relate
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in Db major when playing over Bb minor progressions. The vi-I relationship opens up harmonic possibilities. Perfect for major sections in minor key compositions

compatibleScales.relationship: ii modal scale in Db major system - subdominant minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb Dorian is the second mode of Db major (relative major of Bb minor). Provides subdominant minor function with sophisticated jazzy character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, funk, modal rock. Perfect for creating modal interplay between Bb and Eb minor centers. Common in Santana and Miles Davis style playing
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Ebm7 chords in Bb minor contexts. Creates smooth modal movement between tonic and subdominant. Excellent for ii-i progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: iii modal scale in Db major - mediant minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F Phrygian shares all notes with Bb natural minor and Db major. Provides exotic color when used over F minor chords in the key
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, flamenco-influenced rock, progressive metal. Creates tension and exotic flavor when emphasizing the Gb b2 of F Phrygian
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Fm chords for Phrygian color. Creates interesting modal contrast with Bb minor. Perfect for iii-i progressions with Spanish flavor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Hard Rock, Grunge

compatibleScales.relationship: vii° modal scale - diminished function
compatibleScales.rationale: Ab Locrian contains all notes of Bb minor/Db major but centers on the diminished vii° chord. Creates tension and instability that wants to resolve
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Advanced jazz, progressive rock, modern classical. Rarely used as a primary scale but valuable for creating tension over diminished chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use briefly over Abdim or Abm7b5 chords for tension that resolves to Bbm. Most effective as a passing sonority rather than tonal center

compatibleScales.relationship: 5th mode of harmonic minor - exotic dominant
compatibleScales.rationale: Combines Phrygian b2 with major 3rd, creating Spanish/flamenco dominant character. Contains both Cb (b2) and D (major 3rd) for exotic tension
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, metal, Middle Eastern music, film scores. Signature sound of flamenco guitar and Phrygian-dominant metal riffs. Creates dramatic, exotic atmosphere
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bb7 chords with Spanish or exotic flavor. Essential for flamenco-style cadences. The Cb-D relationship creates characteristic tension
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Metal, Middle Eastern, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: 4th mode of melodic minor - altered major sound
compatibleScales.rationale: Combines Lydian #4 with Mixolydian b7, creating sophisticated dominant color. Contains E natural and Ab for jazz-fusion dominant character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, modern jazz, progressive rock. Signature scale of jazz fusion guitarists like John Scofield and Mike Stern. Creates hip, contemporary dominant sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect over Bb7#11 chords in jazz contexts. Essential for fusion dominant lines. Emphasize the E natural for Lydian dominant characteristic
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Modern Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical augmented scale - all whole steps
compatibleScales.rationale: Six-note scale built entirely of whole steps (Bb-C-D-E-F#-G#), creating ambiguous, floating, dreamlike quality. No perfect 5th creates suspended, unresolved feeling
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist music (Debussy), jazz, film scores, dream sequences. Creates ethereal, mysterious, or surreal atmosphere
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bb7+5 or Bbaug chords for impressionistic color. Perfect for creating tension that needs resolution. Works best in short phrases
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Jazz, Film Score, Contemporary Classical

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical dominant diminished scale
compatibleScales.rationale: Eight-note symmetrical scale with alternating whole-half pattern. Creates tension over dominant 7th chords with rich upper extensions
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, modern jazz, fusion. Essential for jazz improvisation over altered dominant chords. Creates sophisticated, complex harmonic color
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bb7b9 chords for bebop and jazz lines. The symmetry allows easy transposition. Perfect for creating outside tension that resolves

compatibleScales.relationship: All 12 notes - ultimate passing tone source
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains all 12 chromatic notes, providing maximum melodic freedom. Every note can function as either chord tone, tension, or passing tone
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, modern jazz, contemporary classical, atonal music. Used for chromatic approach tones, enclosures, and connecting diatonic phrases
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic passing tones to connect chord tones smoothly. Essential for bebop-style eighth note runs. Creates sophisticated melodic lines with strong voice leading

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset - scale tones 1, 2, 3, 5, 6 only
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains only the most consonant notes of Bb major (Bb, C, D, F, G), avoiding Eb (4th) and A (7th) which create tension. Completely safe for any Bb major harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all genres; creates bright, optimistic melodies. Especially effective in rock, blues, country, folk, and pop music
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for improvisation with guaranteed consonance. Works over I-IV-V progressions in Bb major. Ideal for creating singable melodies and memorable hooks

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode with raised 4th degree (Eb becomes E)
compatibleScales.rationale: Shares all notes except Eb→E, creating a brighter, more ethereal major sound. The #4 creates characteristic dreamy, floating quality
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores, progressive rock, fusion. Common in Joe Satriani and Steve Vai guitar solos. Creates uplifting, transcendent atmosphere
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bbmaj7 or Bbmaj7#11 chords; emphasize the E natural for color. Perfect for I chord vamps and modal compositions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Score, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Dream Pop

compatibleScales.relationship: V chord scale - dominant to tonic relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: F major is the V of Bb major; shares 6 of 7 notes (only Eb→E). Creates natural dominant tension that resolves to Bb
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; essential for V-I cadences. Fundamental to Western harmony from Bach to contemporary music
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when moving to/from F major chords; emphasizes dominant function. Essential for perfect authentic cadences and establishing tonality

compatibleScales.relationship: IV chord scale - subdominant relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb major is the IV of Bb major; shares 6 of 7 notes (only A→Ab). Provides subdominant function and smooth voice leading to tonic
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; essential for I-IV-V progressions. Creates the plagal (IV-I) cadence feeling
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Eb major chords and IV-I progressions. Creates smooth subdominant-tonic movement. Essential for folk, rock, and pop songwriting

compatibleScales.relationship: vi scale - relative minor, shares all notes
compatibleScales.rationale: G natural minor is the relative minor of Bb major, containing the exact same seven notes but centered on G. This vi-I relationship is fundamental
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all styles. The major-minor relationship is essential for understanding modulation and creating emotional contrast
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for switching between major and minor moods without changing key signature. Use over Gm (vi) chords for minor color in major key

compatibleScales.relationship: ii modal scale in Bb major system
compatibleScales.rationale: C Dorian is the second mode of Bb major, shares all notes. Provides sophisticated minor color over the ii chord (Cm7)
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, funk, contemporary styles. More versatile than natural minor due to the major 6th. The go-to minor scale for modern improvisation
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential for ii-V-I progressions in Bb major. Use over Cm7 chords for jazz/funk flavor. Creates smooth modal movement within the key

compatibleScales.relationship: iii modal scale in Bb major - mediant minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D Phrygian shares all notes with Bb major. The characteristic b2 (Eb) creates Spanish/flamenco color over the iii chord
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, flamenco-influenced styles, progressive rock. Creates exotic, dark atmosphere when emphasizing the b2
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Dm chords for Phrygian flavor in Bb major context. Perfect for iii-I progressions with Spanish character

compatibleScales.relationship: V modal scale - dominant 7th function
compatibleScales.rationale: F Mixolydian shares all notes with Bb major. Perfect for the V7 chord (F7), providing the dominant 7th sound needed for resolution
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; essential for dominant function. The foundation of blues, rock, and funk dominant chord vamps
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F7 chords for blues-rock or funk flavor. Essential for V7-I resolutions with b7 character. Perfect for dominant vamps

compatibleScales.relationship: vii° modal scale - diminished function
compatibleScales.rationale: A Locrian shares all notes with Bb major but creates diminished chord tension over the vii° (Adim). Highly unstable, wants to resolve
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Advanced jazz, modern classical. Rarely used as tonal center but valuable for creating tension over half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use briefly over Am7b5 chords for tension resolving to Bbmaj. Most effective as passing sonority in ii-V-I progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode with b7 - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes only A→Ab, creating dominant 7th character. Adds bluesy, funky flavor while maintaining Bb tonal center
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, funk, folk. Signature sound of classic rock I7 vamps and jam band grooves. Perfect for blues-rock with major tonality
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bb7 chords and I7 vamps. The Ab is key - emphasize it for Mixolydian character. Essential for blues-rock solos
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Southern Rock, Country, Americana

compatibleScales.relationship: Major blues with added b3 and #4 blue notes
compatibleScales.rationale: Major pentatonic with blue notes Db (between C and D) and E (between Eb and F), creating that classic blues-rock major sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues-rock, country, Southern rock. Essential for major blues guitar solos and the Allman Brothers/Skynyrd sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bb major and Bb7 chords for blues flavor. Bend into the blue notes for authentic blues feeling. Perfect for major blues solos

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII major - mediant major relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: Db major shares 5 notes with Bb major. Creates bright, uplifting contrast. Common modulation point and provides major bIII chord color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Pop, rock, contemporary Christian music. Popular for key changes and major bIII chord progressions (I-bIII-IV)
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for modulation or over Db major chords (bIII) for contemporary pop sound. Creates smooth transition between closely related keys

compatibleScales.relationship: bVII major - leading tone major relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: Ab major shares 5 notes with Bb major. Creates bright resolution down by whole step. Provides unusual but effective major bVII color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contemporary pop, progressive rock. Creates modern, unexpected harmonic movement when used as bVII instead of vii°
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for contemporary bVII-I progressions or modulation to Ab major. Creates fresh alternative to traditional dominant resolution

compatibleScales.relationship: ii minor pentatonic - subdominant minor function
compatibleScales.rationale: C minor pentatonic (C, Eb, F, G, Bb) fits perfectly over the ii chord and shares 5 notes with Bb major. Provides minor color within major key
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, funk. Common for creating minor flavor over ii chords in major keys. Santana frequently uses this approach
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Cm7 (ii) chords for minor pentatonic flavor in major key context. Perfect for funk and fusion ii-V-I lines

compatibleScales.relationship: Major scale with added #5 passing tone
compatibleScales.rationale: Adds F# (chromatic passing tone) between F and G, creating 8-note scale perfect for bebop eighth-note lines with strong beats on chord tones
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz. Essential for Charlie Parker and Dizzy Gillespie style lines over major chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bebop-style eighth note runs over Bb major chords. The added chromatic note allows chord tones to land on downbeats naturally
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Bebop, Jazz, Hard Bop, Straight-Ahead Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset - scale tones 1, b3, 4, 5, b7 only
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains the core minor pentatonic notes (Bb, Db, Eb, F, Ab), all consonant with Dorian. Removes the characteristic major 6th (G) but remains completely compatible
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal minor sound; works in rock, blues, jazz, funk. The most reliable minor scale for improvisation across all genres
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Safe choice for improvisation over Bb Dorian; impossible to clash. Use when you want straightforward minor sound without modal complexity
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Funk, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent major scale - Bb Dorian is ii mode of Ab major
compatibleScales.rationale: Bb Dorian is the second mode of Ab major system; shares all notes. Understanding this relationship opens up the entire Ab major key center
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; provides major context for Dorian. Think of Bbm7 as ii in Ab major rather than i in Bb minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in Ab major when using Bb Dorian; opens up ii-V-I possibilities in Ab major. Essential for understanding modal relationships

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel minor with b6 - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: One note difference (G→Gb), creating darker, more traditional minor character. Dorian is Aeolian with major 6th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, traditional folk, melancholic rock. More somber than Dorian; the natural minor sound of Aeolian provides darker emotional color
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want traditional, classical minor sound. The Gb creates more tension pulling down to F, while Dorian's G is brighter

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic with added b5 blue note
compatibleScales.rationale: Adds E natural (b5) to minor pentatonic. The blue note creates characteristic blues tension while all other notes align with Dorian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock, jazz-blues. Essential for blues-rock guitar solos over minor chords. Creates authentic blues feeling
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bbm7 or Bb7 chords for blues flavor. Bend into or through the E natural blue note. Perfect for blues-rock improvisation

compatibleScales.relationship: IV modal scale in Ab major - subdominant dominant relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb Mixolydian shares all notes with Bb Dorian (both from Ab major). Creates perfect subdominant-tonic modal movement
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, funk, fusion. Creates sophisticated interplay between Bbm7 (i) and Eb7 (IV) - common in Herbie Hancock style
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for modal vamps alternating between Bb Dorian and Eb Mixolydian. Creates the signature i-IV sound of funk and modal jazz
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Jazz Blues, Funk

compatibleScales.relationship: V chord relationship - dominant to minor tonic
compatibleScales.rationale: F Mixolydian provides dominant function to Bb Dorian tonic. Creates V7-i resolution with modal flavor rather than traditional harmonic minor V-i
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, funk, Latin. The Dorian ii-V sound used extensively by Miles Davis. Softer, less classical than harmonic minor V7-i
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F7 chords resolving to Bbm7. Creates modal jazz V-i instead of classical V7-i. Essential for Dorian-based progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Modal Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode with b2 - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes C→Cb (B), adding Spanish/flamenco character. The b2 creates exotic, darker minor feeling than Dorian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, metal, progressive rock. Creates Spanish or Middle Eastern flavor. Essential for Phrygian-dominant metal riffs
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want Spanish or exotic minor flavor instead of jazz-funk Dorian. The Cb creates characteristic Phrygian sound

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII major - mediant relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: Db major shares 5 notes with Bb Dorian. Creates major color contrast within Dorian context. Common modulation point
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contemporary pop, rock, indie. The i-bIII movement is extremely popular in modern songwriting. Creates uplifting contrast to minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for Bbm to Db major progressions (i-bIII). One of the most popular progressions in contemporary music. Provides bright relief from minor

compatibleScales.relationship: vi modal relationship in Ab major system
compatibleScales.rationale: G Phrygian shares all notes with Bb Dorian (both from Ab major). Provides minor vi chord color with Phrygian character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, progressive rock. Creates interesting minor-to-minor modal movement (i-vi) with contrasting flavors
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Gm chords for Phrygian flavor in Dorian contexts. Creates sophisticated minor-minor modal relationships

compatibleScales.relationship: ii natural minor in Ab major - subdominant minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C natural minor is related to Bb Dorian through the Ab major system. Both are minor scales a whole step apart sharing similar functions
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, metal, classical. Creates darker subdominant minor function. Common for minor ii-i progressions
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Cm chords for darker minor ii sound. Contrasts with the brighter Dorian tonic for dynamic minor progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Metal, Neoclassical, Film Score, Gothic

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel scale with major 6th and 7th
compatibleScales.rationale: Shares major 6th (G) with Dorian but adds major 7th (A). Creates sophisticated jazz minor sound with strong leading tone
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. The foundation of jazz minor harmony and source of altered scales. Essential for modern improvisation
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bbm(maj7) chords for jazz minor color. Opens up melodic minor modal system. More harmonically complex than Dorian
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: 4th mode of harmonic minor - Dorian with raised 4th
compatibleScales.rationale: Bb Dorian with E natural instead of Eb, adding harmonic minor flavor while keeping Dorian's major 6th character. Creates exotic color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, world music, progressive styles. Combines Dorian sophistication with harmonic minor exoticism
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bbm6 or Bbm9 chords for exotic Dorian color. The E natural creates unique harmonic minor-Dorian hybrid sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Hard Bop, Straight-Ahead Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Dorian with added major 3rd passing tone
compatibleScales.rationale: Adds D (major 3rd) between Db and Eb, creating 8-note scale perfect for bebop lines with chord tones on strong beats
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz. Essential for Charlie Parker style minor lines. Creates authentic bebop phrasing
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bebop eighth-note runs over Bbm7 chords. The added chromatic allows natural placement of chord tones on downbeats
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Bebop, Jazz, Hard Bop, Contemporary Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: V Dorian - fifth relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: F Dorian a perfect 5th above creates modal dominant color. Shares 5 notes with Bb Dorian, creating smooth voice leading
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, progressive rock. Creates modal V-i with both chords using Dorian character - less tension than traditional V7-i
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Fm7 chords for modal dominant function. Creates the i-v sound with both using Dorian for consistent modal character

compatibleScales.relationship: IV Dorian - subdominant relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb Dorian a perfect 4th above creates modal subdominant color. Shares multiple notes, creating smooth modal progression
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, funk, fusion. The i-iv modal movement with both chords using Dorian is signature sound of modal funk
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Ebm7 chords for modal subdominant. Creates smooth i-iv movement with consistent Dorian character throughout

compatibleScales.relationship: Compatible minor pentatonic - avoids the characteristic b2
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains Bb, Db, Eb, F, Ab - all consonant with Phrygian. Avoids the Cb (b2) which is Phrygian's most characteristic note
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, metal, blues. Provides safe minor sound without the exotic Phrygian b2. Works when you want standard minor flavor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for straightforward minor improvisation over Bb Phrygian chords. Saves the Cb for emphasis when you want Phrygian character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Thrash Metal, Death Metal, Progressive Metal

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent major scale - Bb Phrygian is iii mode of Gb major
compatibleScales.rationale: Bb Phrygian is the third mode of Gb major; shares all notes. Understanding this relationship reveals the diatonic context
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; provides major context for Phrygian. Think of Bbm as iii in Gb major system rather than Phrygian tonic
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in Gb major when using Bb Phrygian; opens up diatonic iii-I progressions. Essential for understanding modal origins

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel scale with major 3rd - Phrygian with D
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes Db→D (b3 to major 3rd), creating Spanish/flamenco dominant character. Keeps the characteristic b2 (Cb) for Phrygian flavor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, metal, Middle Eastern music. Signature sound of flamenco guitar and Phrygian-dominant metal. Creates dramatic, exotic atmosphere
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bb7 chords with Spanish flavor. Emphasize both the Cb (b2) and D (major 3rd) for authentic flamenco character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Metal, Middle Eastern, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel minor with major 2nd - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes Cb→C (b2 to major 2nd). Natural minor is brighter and less exotic than Phrygian, losing the characteristic b2
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, traditional folk, rock. More traditional minor sound. Use when Phrygian's exotic character is too much
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want standard minor sound without Phrygian's Spanish flavor. The C creates more conventional minor character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Hard Rock, Grunge

compatibleScales.relationship: vi modal scale in Gb major - minor vi function
compatibleScales.rationale: G Dorian shares all notes with Bb Phrygian (both from Gb major). Creates minor vi to iii modal movement
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, funk, progressive rock. The minor vi-iii relationship provides sophisticated modal harmony

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, jazz. Essential for bebop lines in minor keys with harmonic minor harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bebop eighth-note runs over Bbm(maj7). Chord tones land on strong beats

compatibleScales.relationship: V7 chord - dominant resolution
compatibleScales.rationale: F7 is the V7 in Bb harmonic minor. The A natural creates the leading tone for V7-i resolution
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, jazz, all genres. The V7-i cadence is fundamental to minor key harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use F Phrygian Dominant over F7 resolving to Bbm. Essential for minor key cadences

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset - core minor tones
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains Bb, Db, Eb, F, Ab - provides stable minor foundation. Avoids the G and A which give melodic minor its character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal minor sound. Safe foundation before adding melodic minor sophistication
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for basic minor, then add G and A for melodic minor color when desired

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode with b7 instead of major 7th
compatibleScales.rationale: One note difference (A→Ab). Dorian shares the major 6th (G) but has minor 7th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, funk. Dorian is more common in jazz-funk; melodic minor is more sophisticated
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Mix Dorian (Ab) and melodic minor (A) for color variety. Same major 6th character

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode with b6 instead of major 6th
compatibleScales.rationale: One note difference (G→Gb). Harmonic minor has the augmented 2nd interval melodic minor avoids
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, metal. Both share major 7th leading tone. Mix for variety
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Alternate between melodic and harmonic minor for textural variety. Both resolve strongly to Bb

compatibleScales.relationship: 2nd mode - Dorian with b2
compatibleScales.rationale: C Dorian b2 is the 2nd mode of Bb melodic minor. Creates exotic minor with b2 and major 6th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Sophisticated minor sound combining Phrygian b2 with Dorian brightness
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Cm7 for exotic minor color with Phrygian darkness and Dorian sophistication

compatibleScales.relationship: 3rd mode - Lydian with #5
compatibleScales.rationale: Db Lydian Augmented is the 3rd mode of Bb melodic minor. Ultra-bright augmented major
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores. The brightest major scale with both #4 and #5
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Dbmaj7#5 for ethereal augmented major. Creates floating, ascending quality

compatibleScales.relationship: 4th mode - Lydian with b7
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb Lydian Dominant is the 4th mode of Bb melodic minor. Hip jazz-fusion dominant
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, modern jazz. THE scale for #11 dominant chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Eb7#11 chords. Essential for contemporary jazz dominant lines

compatibleScales.relationship: 5th mode - Mixolydian with b6
compatibleScales.rationale: F Mixolydian b6 is the 5th mode of Bb melodic minor. Dominant with minor 6th color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores. Creates bittersweet dominant sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F7 with b13 or F7b6. Creates sophisticated dominant with dark color

compatibleScales.relationship: 6th mode - Half-diminished scale
compatibleScales.rationale: G Aeolian b5 is the 6th mode of Bb melodic minor. The primary half-diminished scale
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. THE scale for m7b5 chords in jazz
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Gm7b5 chords. Essential for ii° in minor ii-V-i progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: 7th mode - Altered dominant scale
compatibleScales.rationale: A Altered is the 7th mode of Bb melodic minor. Contains all altered tensions (b9, #9, #11, b13)
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. THE scale for altered dominant chords (7#9, 7b9, 7#11, etc.)
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A7alt resolving to D. Essential for altered dominant resolution

compatibleScales.relationship: Primary chord - minor major 7th
compatibleScales.rationale: Bbm(maj7) is the tonic chord of melodic minor. The A natural creates the characteristic major 7th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film noir. Creates sophisticated, mysterious minor sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use melodic minor over Bbm(maj7) vamps. The G and A define the melodic minor character

compatibleScales.relationship: Blues scale for hybrid blues-jazz
compatibleScales.rationale: Blues scale adds grit to melodic minor sophistication. The E natural blue note creates additional tension
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz-blues, fusion. Combines jazz minor sophistication with blues feeling
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Mix blues scale with melodic minor for jazz-blues fusion sound

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical diminished alternative
compatibleScales.rationale: Half-whole diminished shares diminished quality. Both provide tension over half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop. Chromatic approach to diminished harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use half-whole for chromatic lines, Locrian ♮6 for diatonic approach

compatibleScales.relationship: Standard Locrian - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes G→Gb. Standard Locrian is darker without the major 6th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, progressive rock. Mix both for variety over half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Locrian ♮6 for brighter m7b5, standard Locrian for darker sound

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - 2nd mode of Cb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Bb Locrian ♮6 is the 2nd mode of Cb harmonic minor. Understanding reveals harmonic context
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, jazz. Essential for understanding the harmonic minor system
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in Cb harmonic minor context. Creates proper voice leading in minor progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: iv modal scale in Cb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb Dorian #4 shares all notes (both from Cb harmonic minor). Creates exotic subdominant
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, world music. Both scales from same harmonic minor system
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Ebm6 in progressions with Bb Locrian ♮6 on Bbm7b5

compatibleScales.relationship: V modal scale in Cb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Gb Phrygian Dominant shares all notes. Creates V7 resolution to Cb minor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, classical. The V7-i cadence using Phrygian dominant
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Bb Locrian ♮6 on ii°, Gb Phrygian Dominant on V7 in Cb minor

compatibleScales.relationship: III modal scale in Cb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Db Ionian #5 shares all notes. Creates augmented major color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores. The III+ chord in harmonic minor progressions
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Dbmaj7#5 in harmonic minor progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: Safe minor subset
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic avoids the characteristic tones. Provides stable foundation
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues. Use for safer improvisation over Bbm7b5
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use pentatonic for safe lines, add Locrian ♮6 character notes for color

compatibleScales.relationship: VI modal scale in Cb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A Lydian #2 shares all notes. Creates exotic major vi chord color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores. Very exotic major sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Amaj7#11 in harmonic minor context

compatibleScales.relationship: VII modal scale in related harmonic system
compatibleScales.rationale: Creates altered dominant tensions. Both scales provide sophisticated harmonic color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. Advanced dominant scale for altered voicings
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for altered dominant lines with b9 and b13

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel scale from melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Aeolian b5 (Locrian #2) has natural 2nd. Different approach to half-diminished
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. Two options for m7b5 - Locrian ♮6 from harmonic, Aeolian b5 from melodic
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Compare Locrian ♮6 (b2, ♮6) vs Aeolian b5 (♮2, b6) over Bbm7b5

compatibleScales.relationship: Major pentatonic avoiding #5
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains Bb, C, D, F, G - avoids the F# (#5) which is Ionian #5's characteristic note
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Pop, rock. Provides stable major foundation without augmented tension
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use pentatonic for safe major lines, add F# for augmented color when desired

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel scale with #4 instead of natural 4
compatibleScales.rationale: Lydian Augmented has E natural (#4) instead of Eb. Both share the #5 (F#)
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores. Both create ethereal augmented major. Different 4th degree
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Compare Ionian #5 (natural 4) vs Lydian Aug (#4). Both work over maj7#5

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - 3rd mode of G harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Bb Ionian #5 is the 3rd mode of G harmonic minor. Essential for understanding context
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, jazz. The III chord in harmonic minor has augmented quality
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in G harmonic minor context. Bbmaj7#5 is the bIII+ chord

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical augmented alternative
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole tone also creates augmented chord sound. More symmetrical and ambiguous
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist, jazz. Whole tone is more floating, Ionian #5 is more diatonic
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Ionian #5 for diatonic augmented, whole tone for symmetrical ambiguity

compatibleScales.relationship: V modal scale in G harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D Phrygian Dominant shares all notes (both from G harmonic minor). Creates V7 tension
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, classical. The V7 resolving to Gm in harmonic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D7 in G harmonic minor progressions involving Bbmaj7#5

compatibleScales.relationship: ii modal scale in G harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A Dorian #4 shares all notes. Creates exotic ii chord in G minor context
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, world music. Exotic minor ii chord sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Am6 in G harmonic minor progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: vii modal scale in G harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C Locrian ♮6 shares all notes. Creates half-diminished vii chord
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, classical. The vii°7 chord function in harmonic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Cm7b5 resolving in G harmonic minor progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: Standard Ionian with natural 5th
compatibleScales.rationale: One note difference (F#→F). Standard major without augmented character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres. Use when augmented tension is not desired
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Alternate between Ionian #5 (F#) and standard Ionian (F) for textural variety

compatibleScales.relationship: VI modal scale in G harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb Lydian #2 shares all notes. Creates exotic major bVI chord
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores. Very exotic major sound with #2 and #4
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Ebmaj7#11 in G harmonic minor context

compatibleScales.relationship: Related altered dominant scale
compatibleScales.rationale: Creates altered dominant tensions over F#7. Complex harmonic relationship
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. Advanced altered dominant applications
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated altered dominant lines with b9 and b13

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic avoiding #4
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains core minor tones (Bb, Db, Eb, F, Ab). Avoids the E natural (#4) characteristic
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues. Safe minor foundation before adding exotic #4
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use pentatonic for safe lines, add E natural for Dorian #4 color

compatibleScales.relationship: Standard Dorian with natural 4th
compatibleScales.rationale: One note difference (E→Eb). Standard Dorian without the exotic #4
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, funk. Mix both for variety - Dorian for standard, Dorian #4 for exotic
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Alternate between Dorian (Eb) and Dorian #4 (E) for color variety

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - 4th mode of F harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Bb Dorian #4 is the 4th mode of F harmonic minor. Essential context
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, jazz. The iv chord in F harmonic minor has Dorian #4 quality
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in F harmonic minor context. Bbm6 is the iv chord

compatibleScales.relationship: V modal scale in F harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C Phrygian Dominant shares all notes. Creates V7 resolution to Fm
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, classical. The V7-i cadence in F minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C7 resolving to Fm. Common progression with Bb Dorian #4 on iv

compatibleScales.relationship: III modal scale in F harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Ab Ionian #5 shares all notes. Creates augmented bIII chord

compatibleScales.rationale: E Locrian ♮6 shares all notes. Creates half-diminished vii chord

compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for altered dominant lines with exotic tensions

compatibleScales.relationship: Blues scale for hybrid sound
compatibleScales.rationale: Blues scale's b5 (E natural) matches Dorian #4's #4 (same note). Creates interesting hybrid
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz-blues, fusion. The E natural works as both blue note and #4
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Mix blues scale with Dorian #4 - E natural serves both functions

compatibleScales.relationship: Related harmonic minor - shares exotic color
compatibleScales.rationale: Both come from harmonic minor systems. Share exotic, classical minor character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, metal. Both have that neoclassical harmonic minor flavor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Mix Dorian #4 and harmonic minor for rich neoclassical minor palette

compatibleScales.relationship: Standard Mixolydian with natural 2nd and 6th
compatibleScales.rationale: Mixolydian has C and G (natural 2/6) vs Phrygian Dom's Cb and Gb (b2/b6). Different dominant flavors
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues vs flamenco, metal. Mixolydian for blues-rock, Phrygian Dom for Spanish
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Mixolydian for blues-rock dominant, Phrygian Dominant for exotic Spanish dominant

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - 5th mode of Eb harmonic minor

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, jazz, flamenco. Essential for V7-i in minor keys

compatibleScales.rationale: One note difference (D→Db). Phrygian is minor, Phrygian Dominant is dominant
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Metal, flamenco. Mix both for Spanish/Middle Eastern color palette

compatibleScales.relationship: Tonic minor pentatonic for resolution
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb minor pentatonic is the resolution target. Bb Phrygian Dominant creates tension to Eb minor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres. The V7-i relationship with pentatonic resolution
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Bb Phrygian Dominant on V7, resolve to Eb minor pentatonic on i

compatibleScales.relationship: ii modal scale in Eb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F Dorian #4 shares all notes. Creates exotic ii chord before V7

compatibleScales.relationship: vii modal scale in Eb harmonic minor

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. The vii°7 in Eb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Dm7b5 resolving in Eb harmonic minor progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical alternative for dominant
compatibleScales.rationale: Half-whole diminished provides chromatic dominant tensions. More symmetrical approach

compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use half-whole for bebop chromatic lines over Bb7, Phrygian Dom for diatonic Spanish

compatibleScales.relationship: Primary chord - dominant 7th
compatibleScales.rationale: Bb7 is the chord Phrygian Dominant is built on. The b9 (Cb) and b13 (Gb) are characteristic tensions
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres with minor keys. THE V7 chord in Eb minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bb7 or Bb7b9 resolving to Ebm. Essential for minor key cadences

compatibleScales.relationship: Standard Lydian with natural 2nd
compatibleScales.rationale: One note difference (C#→C). Lydian #2 has raised 2nd, standard Lydian has natural 2nd
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores. Lydian #2 is more exotic, standard Lydian is more common

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - 6th mode of Gb harmonic minor

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, jazz. The bVI in Gb harmonic minor progressions
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in Gb harmonic minor context. Bbmaj7#11 is the bVI chord

compatibleScales.relationship: Major pentatonic avoiding exotic tones
compatibleScales.rationale: Major pentatonic avoids both #2 (C#) and #4 (E). Provides stable major foundation
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Pop, rock. Safe major before adding exotic Lydian #2 color

compatibleScales.relationship: V modal scale in Gb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Db Phrygian Dominant shares all notes. Creates V7 in Gb minor context
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, classical. The V7-i using harmonic minor modes
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Db7 resolving to Gbm in harmonic minor progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: ii modal scale in Gb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Ab Dorian #4 shares all notes. Creates exotic ii chord
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, world music. The exotic ii in harmonic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Abm6 in Gb harmonic minor progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: III modal scale in Gb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A Ionian #5 shares all notes. Creates augmented III chord

compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Amaj7#5 in Gb harmonic minor context

compatibleScales.relationship: vii modal scale in Gb harmonic minor

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. The vii°7 chord in Gb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Fm7b5 in Gb harmonic minor progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: Related altered dominant

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. Advanced altered dominant applications
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for altered dominant lines with exotic tensions

compatibleScales.rationale: Both have bright, ethereal quality. Whole tone is symmetrical, Lydian #2 is diatonic
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist, jazz. Both create floating, bright atmosphere
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use whole tone for symmetrical ambiguity, Lydian #2 for diatonic exotic brightness

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical scale from #2
compatibleScales.rationale: Half-whole from C# creates interesting tensions. Complex but usable for outside playing
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Avant-garde jazz. Creates complex chromatic tension
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for outside playing over Lydian #2 context. Very advanced application

compatibleScales.relationship: Same scale - alternative name
compatibleScales.rationale: Mixolydian b9 b13 and Phrygian Dominant are the same scale. Both are 5th mode of harmonic minor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, flamenco, jazz. THE V7 scale in minor keys

compatibleScales.relationship: Standard Mixolydian with natural 9 and 13
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes Cb→C and Gb→G. Standard Mixolydian for major key V7, this for minor key V7

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - 5th mode of Eb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Same as Phrygian Dominant. THE V7 in Eb minor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, jazz. Essential for minor key V7-i
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bb7 resolving to Ebm. Essential minor key cadence

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical dominant alternative
compatibleScales.rationale: Half-whole provides additional chromatic tensions. More symmetrical than Mixolydian b9 b13
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, jazz. Creates chromatic V7 lines
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use half-whole for bebop chromatic lines, Mixolydian b9 b13 for diatonic approach

compatibleScales.relationship: Related altered dominant
compatibleScales.rationale: Altered scale provides different set of tensions (#9, b9, #11, b13). Both for V7 in minor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. Both work over V7 resolving to minor - different color options

compatibleScales.relationship: Resolution target pentatonic
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb minor pentatonic is the resolution. Creates V7-i movement
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres. The classic V7 to minor pentatonic resolution
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Mixolydian b9 b13 on V7, resolve to Eb minor pentatonic

compatibleScales.relationship: iv modal scale in Eb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F Dorian #4 shares all notes. Creates exotic iv chord
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, flamenco. The ii-V-i using harmonic minor modes
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Fm6 before Bb7 (Mixolydian b9 b13) resolving to Ebm

compatibleScales.relationship: III modal scale in Eb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Gb Ionian #5 shares all notes. Creates augmented bIII
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores. The III+ chord in harmonic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Gbmaj7#5 in Eb harmonic minor progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: vii modal scale in Eb harmonic minor

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. The vii°7 in Eb harmonic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Dm7b5 in Eb harmonic minor progressions

compatibleScales.relationship: Primary chord - dominant 7th b9

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, classical. Standard V7b9 voicing in minor keys
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bb7b9 or Bb7b9b13 resolving to Ebm

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - Bb Dorian b2 is the second mode of Ab melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Bb Dorian b2 is built from the second degree of Ab melodic minor. This parent-mode relationship is fundamental to understanding melodic minor modal harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. Dorian b2 combines minor quality with both dark (b2) and bright (major 6th) characteristics, creating sophisticated minor color
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bbm7 or Bbm9 chords when the harmonic center is Ab melodic minor. The b2 (Cb/B) provides Phrygian darkness while the major 6th (G) maintains Dorian sophistication
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 2nd degree (C vs Cb)
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard Dorian has major 2nd (C) while Dorian b2 has minor 2nd (Cb/B). The b2 adds Phrygian darkness to Dorian's otherwise bright minor character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, funk, fusion. Standard Dorian is more common and versatile. Dorian b2 provides exotic alternative when you want darker, more Spanish-influenced minor sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use standard Dorian for typical jazz/funk minor. Switch to Dorian b2 for darker, more exotic flavor with Spanish/Phrygian character

compatibleScales.relationship: Closely related - differs only in the 6th degree (Gb vs G)
compatibleScales.rationale: Phrygian has b6 (Gb) while Dorian b2 has major 6th (G). Both share the dark b2, but Dorian b2 is brighter due to the major 6th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Spanish music, metal, jazz. Phrygian is darker and more traditional Spanish. Dorian b2 is jazzier with its major 6th
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Phrygian for traditional Spanish/flamenco darkness. Use Dorian b2 for jazz/fusion minor with Spanish flavor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Thrash Metal, Death Metal, Progressive Metal

compatibleScales.relationship: Simplified minor framework avoiding the b2
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic provides safe minor sound without the exotic b2. Uses major 2nd (C) and avoids Dorian b2's Phrygian character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, folk. When Dorian b2 sounds too exotic or jazzy, minor pentatonic offers familiar, accessible minor vocabulary
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use minor pentatonic for straightforward rock/blues minor. Dorian b2 adds sophistication when the musical context supports it
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Grunge, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: II augmented - third mode of Ab melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C Lydian Augmented is the third mode of Ab melodic minor. Combines Lydian #4 with augmented 5th for maximum brightness, connecting through shared parent scale
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. Extremely bright augmented major sound typical of melodic minor system
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Cmaj7#5 (II) for bright augmented color. Understanding this modal relationship reveals melodic minor system connections
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: III dominant - fourth mode of Ab melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D Lydian Dominant (fourth mode) is the signature melodic minor dominant. Combines Lydian #4 with Mixolydian b7 for jazz fusion sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, contemporary jazz. The most important melodic minor mode for modern jazz dominant function
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D7#11 (III7) for fusion dominant. The #4 and b7 combination defines modern jazz dominant sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Progressive Rock, Instrumental

compatibleScales.relationship: IV dominant - fifth mode of Ab melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb Mixolydian b6 (fifth mode) creates dark sophisticated dominant. The minor 6th provides unique tension unavailable in standard Mixolydian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, world music. Creates darker dominant than Mixolydian, essential for melodic minor V7-i resolution
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Eb7 (IV7) for sophisticated dark dominant. The Cb (b6) creates characteristic melodic minor tension

compatibleScales.relationship: V half-diminished - sixth mode of Ab melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F Locrian #2 (sixth mode) is the go-to half-diminished scale. Major 2nd makes it far more usable than standard Locrian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. Essential for half-diminished chords in jazz. More melodic than standard Locrian
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Fm7b5 (V) for jazz half-diminished sound. The major 2nd (G) makes it more melodic than Locrian
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: VI altered dominant - seventh mode of Ab melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: G Super Locrian is THE jazz altered dominant scale. Contains all altered extensions (b9, #9, #11, b13) essential for bebop and modern jazz
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop, fusion. The signature altered dominant essential for modern jazz harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential over G7alt (VI7alt) for maximum alteration. The most important scale for jazz altered dominant chords

compatibleScales.relationship: Same parent scale - understanding the melodic minor system
compatibleScales.rationale: Understanding Ab melodic minor as the source of Dorian b2 is essential. In jazz, melodic minor is used both ascending and descending
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary. The melodic minor system is the foundation of modern jazz harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in terms of Ab melodic minor when using Dorian b2. Opens up the entire melodic minor modal system
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Dorian with added major 3rd for bebop lines
compatibleScales.rationale: Bebop Dorian adds D between Db and Eb, creating 8-note scale. Combines with Dorian b2 for chromatic bebop minor vocabulary
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz. Essential for Charlie Parker/Bud Powell style minor lines
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bebop eighth-note runs over Bbm7. Alternate with Dorian b2 for exotic flavor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Hard Bop, Contemporary Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII major - mediant relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: Db major shares several notes with Bb Dorian b2. The bIII major provides bright contrast in minor contexts
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, rock, pop. The i-bIII progression is common for major relief in minor keys
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Db major (bIII) for bright major color. Common modulation point from minor

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - Bb Lydian Augmented is the third mode of Gb melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Bb Lydian Augmented derives from the third degree of Gb melodic minor. Combines Lydian's #4 with augmented 5th, creating the brightest possible major scale sonority
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical, film scores. One of the brightest scales in existence, used for maximum major-key luminosity and floating augmented harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bbmaj7#5 or Bbmaj7#11#5 chords when the harmonic center is Gb melodic minor. Both E (#4) and F# (aug 5th) create characteristic bright, suspended quality
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 5th degree (F vs F#)
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard Lydian has natural 5th while Lydian Augmented has augmented 5th. Both share the characteristic bright #4, but augmented 5th adds suspension and ambiguity
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores, fusion. Lydian is more stable and usable. Lydian Augmented provides maximum brightness when suspension is desired
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Lydian for stable bright major sound. Use Lydian Augmented when you want floating, suspended augmented quality with #4 brightness
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Score, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Dream Pop

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical scale sharing augmented triad core
compatibleScales.rationale: Both contain Bb augmented triad (Bb-D-F#). Augmented scale provides symmetrical chromatic approach while Lydian Augmented offers diatonic structure
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical, avant-garde. Augmented scale's symmetry complements Lydian Augmented's diatonic brightness
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use augmented scale for chromatic symmetrical approach over Bb aug. Use Lydian Augmented for diatonic melodic approach. Both work over maj7#5 chords

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical scale sharing augmented quality and brightness
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole tone's complete symmetry and augmented intervals relate to Lydian Augmented's augmented 5th. Both create suspended, floating major harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist classical (Debussy), jazz, film scores. Whole tone provides maximum ambiguity, Lydian Augmented maintains diatonic function
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use whole tone for completely ambiguous floating sound. Use Lydian Augmented when you want bright suspended sound with diatonic melodic structure
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Contemporary Classical, Film Score, Ambient

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale understanding - melodic minor system
compatibleScales.rationale: Understanding Gb melodic minor as the source reveals the entire modal system. Lydian Augmented as III creates the brightest color in melodic minor harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary. Melodic minor system is foundation of modern jazz harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in Gb melodic minor when using Lydian Augmented. Opens up entire melodic minor modal possibilities
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: II - second mode of Gb melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Ab Dorian b2 shares Gb melodic minor parent. Creates ii-III progression using both modes of the melodic minor system
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. The ii-III progression explores different colors within the same melodic minor parent
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Abm7 (ii) before Bbmaj7#5 (III). Explores melodic minor system through connected modal movement
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: bII dominant - fourth mode of Gb melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Cb Lydian Dominant (fourth mode) is the signature melodic minor dominant. Creates tritone relationship with Lydian Augmented
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, contemporary jazz. Essential for modern jazz dominant function and melodic minor harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Cb7#11 (bII7) for fusion dominant. The tritone substitution relationship creates sophisticated harmonic movement
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Progressive Rock, Instrumental

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII dominant - fifth mode of Gb melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Db Mixolydian b6 provides dark dominant color. Shares Gb melodic minor parent scale
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, world music. Dark sophisticated dominant characteristic of melodic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Db7 (bIII7) for dark dominant within Gb melodic minor system

compatibleScales.relationship: IV half-diminished - sixth mode of Gb melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb Locrian #2 shares the parent scale. Creates half-diminished color within melodic minor system
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Essential half-diminished scale for jazz
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Ebm7b5 (IV) within Gb melodic minor progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: V altered - seventh mode of Gb melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F Super Locrian is THE altered dominant. Contains all alterations for bebop/jazz
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop, fusion. Essential for altered dominant chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F7alt (V7alt) for maximum alteration in Gb melodic minor context

compatibleScales.relationship: Pentatonic subset avoiding the #4 and #5
compatibleScales.rationale: Major pentatonic provides safe, consonant major sound. Lydian Augmented adds exotic #4 and #5 for sophisticated color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres. Major pentatonic for accessible major, Lydian Augmented for bright sophistication
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use major pentatonic for simple major sound. Use Lydian Augmented for maximum brightness over aug maj7

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - Bb Lydian Dominant is the fourth mode (IV7) of F melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Bb Lydian Dominant is THE most important melodic minor mode for modern jazz. Combines Lydian's bright #4 (E) with Mixolydian's dominant b7 (Ab), creating the signature jazz fusion sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, contemporary jazz, progressive rock. The defining scale for modern dominant 7th harmony. Used extensively by Scofield, Stern, Metheny, Herbie Hancock, and all modern jazz masters
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential over Bb7#11, Bb9#11, Bb13#11 for fusion sound. The #4 (E) over dominant 7th chord is the signature fusion tension. Works beautifully as tritone sub or IV7 in F melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Progressive Rock, Instrumental

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 4th degree (Eb vs E)
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard Mixolydian has perfect 4th while Lydian Dominant has raised 4th. The #4 adds brightness and sophistication to the dominant sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues/rock vs jazz/fusion. Mixolydian for traditional blues/rock, Lydian Dominant for jazz fusion sophistication
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Mixolydian for blues/rock dominant. Use Lydian Dominant for modern jazz/fusion dominant with #11 sophistication
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Southern Rock, Country, Jam Band

compatibleScales.relationship: Both share #4 - different 7th degree
compatibleScales.rationale: Both Lydian and Lydian Dominant share the characteristic #4 (E). Lydian has major 7th, Lydian Dominant has minor 7th (dominant)
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Lydian for major 7th chords, Lydian Dominant for dominant 7th chords. Both provide #4 brightness
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Lydian over maj7 chords, Lydian Dominant over dominant 7th chords. The #4 brightness works in both contexts
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Score, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Dream Pop

compatibleScales.relationship: Dominant with added major 7th for bebop lines
compatibleScales.rationale: Bebop dominant adds A between Ab and Bb, creating 8-note scale. Perfect for bebop lines over dominant 7th chords with chord tones on downbeats
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz. Essential for Charlie Parker/Dizzy Gillespie style dominant lines
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bebop eighth-note runs over Bb7. Combines with Lydian Dominant for modern jazz vocabulary
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Contemporary Jazz, Hard Bop

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale understanding - melodic minor system foundation
compatibleScales.rationale: Understanding F melodic minor as source is essential. Lydian Dominant as IV7 is the most important scale in modern jazz dominant harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. The melodic minor system revolutionized modern jazz harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in F melodic minor when using Lydian Dominant. Opens entire melodic minor modal system
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: II - second mode of F melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: G Dorian b2 shares F melodic minor parent. Creates ii-IV7 progression within melodic minor system
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Minor ii to Lydian Dominant IV7 is common in melodic minor progressions
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Gm7 (ii) before Bb7#11 (IV7). Explores connected modal colors
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: III augmented - third mode of F melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A Lydian Augmented (third mode) provides maximum brightness. Shares melodic minor parent
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. Extremely bright augmented major
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Amaj7#5 (III) for bright augmented. Modal connection within melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: V dominant - fifth mode of F melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C Mixolydian b6 provides dark dominant. The V7 to IV7 progression uses both dominant scales
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Dark sophisticated dominant typical of melodic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C7 (V7) within F melodic minor. Creates modal variety with Lydian Dominant

compatibleScales.relationship: VI half-diminished - sixth mode of F melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D Locrian #2 shares melodic minor parent. Essential half-diminished scale
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Go-to scale for half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Dm7b5 (VI) in F melodic minor context
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: VII altered - seventh mode of F melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: E Super Locrian is the altered dominant. Maximum alteration for jazz
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop. Essential for altered dominant chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E7alt (VII7alt) for complete alteration

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical scale over dominant with #4
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole tone contains #4 and provides completely ambiguous dominant sound. Alternative to Lydian Dominant's diatonic approach
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist, jazz, avant-garde. Maximum ambiguity over dominant chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use whole tone for completely suspended dominant sound. Lydian Dominant for diatonic fusion approach
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Contemporary Classical, Film Score, Ambient

compatibleScales.relationship: IV major - emphasizes the characteristic #4
compatibleScales.rationale: E major highlights the defining #4 of Lydian Dominant. Provides major color at the #4 degree
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Creates interesting harmonic color
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E major (IV) to emphasize the #4 character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Instrumental Rock
